regions.text: A man living in a city today is more like a cell in a complex organism. Other people do almost everything for him. Workers make things and stores sell them.
Other people teach in schools, care for the ill in hospitals. Police and fire stations watch the city. Public services provide light, water, and waste removal.
In exchange, each does his own sort of work, his daily round. police: those who see laws are kept. public: done for the good of all. service: work for others.
upkeep: keeping things ready to be used. daily: every day.
